title: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design
summary: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design retrieves prior skills in an original independent multi-browser form release decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css form inheritance, css form layout, css form focus, css form errors, css form native custom, css form autofill motion in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent multi-browser form release decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey form interface here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the resilient form design system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: cross-browser survey control typography audit · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-browser form release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent multi-browser form release decision.

content.title: independent multi-browser form release decision evidence
content.text: For Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design, choose the css form inheritance claim that preserves the independent multi-browser form release decision purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 1: form controls inherit every font and color property automatically.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 1: inspect native computed styles, normalize only shared design-system properties, and retest every control type and state across browsers.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 1: apply appearance none to every input select and button before deciding how each control works.

options.text: independent multi-browser form release decision phase 2.1: map label control guidance error and action order
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 2.2: set readable measures intrinsic widths and generous targets
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 2.3: allow text controls and groups to wrap without reordering
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 2.4: test touch keyboard zoom translation and narrow widths

steps.title: 03 · predict 3: survey focus indicator clipping incident · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-browser form release decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in independent multi-browser form release decision.

hints: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 3: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 3: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 3: removing outlines is safe when hover styling exists.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 3: create a distinct focus-visible indicator, reserve unclipped space, reinforce groups carefully, and test keyboard plus forced-color paths.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 3: use a color-only background change under hover and focus because both states mean selection.

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent multi-browser form release decision.

Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 4: red borders alone provide clear error recovery.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 4: give each state distinct text and semantics plus visual treatment, time it appropriately, and test correction and repeated submission.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 4: reduce disabled control opacity until its label and value are no longer readable.

hints: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 5: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 5: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 5: custom checkboxes need only a hidden native input and decorative box.

Independent multi-browser form release decision case 5: start with the native control, use accent-color or bounded appearance changes, and customize only with complete semantic and behavioral evidence.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 5: set display none on the real checkbox and attach clicks to a decorative span.

hints: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 6: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 6: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 6: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 6: autofill colors can be overridden without checking readability or user expectations.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 6: inspect browser states, preserve readable data and labels, keep overrides bounded, and remove nonessential transitions under reduced motion.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 6: animate every invalid field continuously because motion guarantees discovery.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 8: a compact desktop form can be proportionally shrunk for touch screens.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 8: design from content order and target needs, use intrinsic block layout, and verify no horizontal scrolling at narrow width and zoom.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 8: use a transform scale on the entire form to create the mobile version.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 26: a compact desktop form can be proportionally shrunk for touch screens.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 26: design from content order and target needs, use intrinsic block layout, and verify no horizontal scrolling at narrow width and zoom.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 26: use a transform scale on the entire form to create the mobile version.

options.text: independent multi-browser form release decision phase 27.1: tab through every control and identify indicator loss or clipping
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 27.2: create a high-contrast focus-visible boundary and offset
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 27.3: add focus-within group context without hiding the control ring
independent multi-browser form release decision phase 27.4: test zoom overflow forced colors themes and validation states

Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 28: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 28: red borders alone provide clear error recovery.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 28: give each state distinct text and semantics plus visual treatment, time it appropriately, and test correction and repeated submission.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 28: reduce disabled control opacity until its label and value are no longer readable.

Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 29: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 29: custom checkboxes need only a hidden native input and decorative box.

Independent multi-browser form release decision case 29: start with the native control, use accent-color or bounded appearance changes, and customize only with complete semantic and behavioral evidence.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 29: set display none on the real checkbox and attach clicks to a decorative span.

content.text: For Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design, choose the css form autofill motion claim that preserves the independent multi-browser form release decision purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 30: identify the user task and data relationship first.

Mastery Exam: Resilient Form Interface Design checkpoint 30: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent multi-browser form release decision case 30: autofill colors can be overridden without checking readability or user expectations.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 30: inspect browser states, preserve readable data and labels, keep overrides bounded, and remove nonessential transitions under reduced motion.
Independent multi-browser form release decision case 30: animate every invalid field continuously because motion guarantees discovery.
